1→--- 2→title: "Introduction" 3→description: "Code Insights is a free, open-source CLI that parses AI coding sessions from Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, and Copilot into a local knowledge base." 4→--- 5→ 6→**Code Insights** is a free, open-source CLI tool and embedded dashboard for analyzing AI coding sessions. 7→ 8→It parses session history from multiple AI coding tools — Claude Code, Cursor, Codex CLI, Copilot CLI, and VS Code Copilot Chat — stores structured data in a local SQLite database, and provides both terminal analytics and a browser-based dashboard with LLM-powered insights. 9→ 10→## How does Code Insights work? 11→ 12→ 13→ 14→## What can Code Insights do? 15→ 16→- **Session browser** — three-panel layout with project nav, session list, and detail view with insights, prompt quality scoring, and full conversation replay 17→- **AI-powered insights** — decisions (with reasoning and alternatives), learnings (with root cause analysis), techniques, and prompt quality analysis 18→- **Insights page** — browse all insights across sessions with Timeline, By Type, By Project, and By Session views 19→- **Analytics** — activity charts, model distribution, cost breakdown, project table 20→- **Export** — quick session export to Markdown, Obsidian, or Notion, plus AI-powered synthesis across sessions into Agent Rules, Knowledge Briefs, and more 21→- **Terminal stats** — `code-insights stats` for quick metrics without opening the browser 22→ 23→## Which AI coding tools are supported? 24→ 25→| Tool | Auto-detected from | 26→|------|--------------------| 27→| Claude Code | `~/.claude/projects/**/*.jsonl` | 28→| Cursor | Platform workspace storage (macOS/Linux/Windows) | 29→| Codex CLI | `~/.codex/sessions/` | 30→| Copilot CLI | `~/.copilot/session-state/` | 31→| VS Code Copilot Chat | VS Code `workspaceStorage/` chat sessions | 32→ 33→## Is my data private? 34→ 35→Everything runs locally. No accounts, no cloud services, no data leaves your machine. The database lives at `~/.code-insights/data.db` and is plain SQLite — you can open it with any SQLite browser. 36→ 37→## Next steps 38→ 39→Continue to [Installation](/docs/getting-started/installation) to get up and running in 60 seconds. 40→

1→---
     2→title: "Installation"
     3→description: "Install the Code Insights CLI globally with npm. Requires Node.js 18+. No configuration files, API keys, or cloud accounts needed."
     4→---
     5→
     6→## Prerequisites
     7→
     8→- Node.js 18 or later
     9→- At least one AI coding tool (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex CLI, Copilot CLI, or VS Code Copilot Chat)
    10→
    11→## Install
    12→
    13→```bash
    14→npm install -g @code-insights/cli
    15→```
    16→
    17→## Verify
    18→
    19→```bash
    20→code-insights --version
    21→```
    22→
    23→That's it. No Firebase, no Google account, no configuration file required.
    24→
    25→## Next steps
    26→
    27→Continue to [Quick Start](/docs/getting-started/quick-start) to sync your sessions and open the dashboard.
    28→

1→---
     2→title: "Quick Start"
     3→description: "Initialize Code Insights, sync your AI coding sessions, and open the local dashboard in under 5 minutes. No accounts or API keys required."
     4→---
     5→
     6→## 1. Initialize
     7→
     8→```bash
     9→code-insights init
    10→```
    11→
    12→The CLI auto-detects your AI coding tools and sets up the local database. No accounts or API keys needed.
    13→
    14→## 2. Sync your sessions
    15→
    16→```bash
    17→code-insights sync
    18→```
    19→
    20→Discovers sessions from all supported tools (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex CLI, Copilot CLI) and stores them in your local SQLite database.
    21→
    22→<Callout type="note">
    23→The first sync processes your entire session history and may take a minute. Subsequent syncs are incremental.
    24→</Callout>
    25→
    26→## 3. Open the dashboard
    27→
    28→```bash
    29→code-insights dashboard
    30→```
    31→
    32→Your browser opens at `localhost:7890` with your synced sessions, activity charts, and stats.
    33→
    34→## 4. Set up auto-sync (optional)
    35→
    36→```bash
    37→code-insights install-hook
    38→```
    39→
    40→Installs a hook that runs `code-insights sync -q` whenever a Claude Code session ends.
    41→
    42→## 5. Configure LLM for insights (optional)
    43→
    44→1. In the dashboard, click **Settings** (gear icon)
    45→2. Select an LLM provider (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, or Ollama for fully local inference)
    46→3. Enter your API key
    47→4. Return to any session and click **Analyze**
    48→
    49→## What's next
    50→
    51→- [The Dashboard](/docs/guides/dashboard) — Tour of the embedded dashboard
    52→- [Terminal Analytics](/docs/guides/terminal-analytics) — Stats in your terminal without the dashboard
    53→- [Insights & Analysis](/docs/guides/insights-analysis) — Deep dive into LLM-powered insights
    54→- [CLI Commands](/docs/reference/cli-commands) — Every command and flag
    55→

1→---
     2→title: "CLI Commands"
     3→description: "Complete reference for all code-insights CLI commands and flags — init, sync, stats, dashboard, config, reset, install-hook, and more."
     4→---
     5→
     6→## Global
     7→
     8→```bash
     9→code-insights --version    # Print version
    10→code-insights --help       # Show help
    11→```
    12→
    13→## init
    14→
    15→```bash
    16→code-insights init
    17→```
    18→
    19→Interactive setup. Detects installed AI coding tools and initializes the local database at `~/.code-insights/data.db`.
    20→
    21→## sync
    22→
    23→```bash
    24→code-insights sync [options]
    25→```
    26→
    27→Syncs sessions from all supported source tools to the local SQLite database.
    28→
    29→| Flag | Description |
    30→|------|-------------|
    31→| `--force` | Re-sync all sessions (ignores modification times) |
    32→| `--dry-run` | Preview what would be synced without writing |
    33→| `-q, --quiet` | Suppress all output |
    34→| `--source <tool>` | Sync only from: `claude-code`, `cursor`, `codex-cli`, `copilot-cli`, `copilot` |
    35→
    36→## open
    37→
    38→```bash
    39→code-insights open [options]
    40→```
    41→
    42→Opens the local dashboard in your default browser. If the dashboard server is already running, this opens a new browser tab to it.
    43→
    44→| Flag | Description |
    45→|------|-------------|
    46→| `--project` | Open filtered to the current project |
    47→
    48→## dashboard
    49→
    50→```bash
    51→code-insights dashboard [options]
    52→```
    53→
    54→Starts the embedded Hono server and opens `localhost:7890` in your browser.
    55→
    56→| Flag | Default | Description |
    57→|------|---------|-------------|
    58→| `-p, --port <number>` | `7890` | Port number for the dashboard server |
    59→| `--no-open` | — | Do not open browser automatically |
    60→
    61→## status
    62→
    63→```bash
    64→code-insights status
    65→```
    66→
    67→Shows sync statistics: total sessions, messages, last sync time, and source tool breakdown.
    68→
    69→## stats
    70→
    71→```bash
    72→code-insights stats [subcommand] [options]
    73→```
    74→
    75→Terminal analytics. See [Terminal Analytics](/docs/guides/terminal-analytics) for details.
    76→
    77→| Subcommand | Description |
    78→|------------|-------------|
    79→| *(none)* | Overview dashboard |
    80→| `cost` | Cost breakdown by project and model |
    81→| `projects` | Per-project detail cards |
    82→| `today` | Today's sessions |
    83→| `models` | Model usage distribution |
    84→
    85→**Shared flags:**
    86→
    87→| Flag | Default | Description |
    88→|------|---------|-------------|
    89→| `--period 7d\|30d\|90d\|all` | `7d` | Time range |
    90→| `--project <name>` | — | Scope to a specific project |
    91→| `--source <tool>` | — | Filter by source tool |
    92→| `--no-sync` | — | Skip auto-sync before showing stats |
    93→
    94→## config
    95→
    96→```bash
    97→code-insights config
    98→```
    99→
   100→Shows the current configuration summary (sync paths, dashboard port, LLM provider, telemetry status).
   101→
   102→### config set
   103→
   104→```bash
   105→code-insights config set <key> <value>
   106→```
   107→
   108→Set a configuration value. Currently supported keys:
   109→
   110→| Key | Values | Description |
   111→|-----|--------|-------------|
   112→| `telemetry` | `true` / `false` | Enable or disable anonymous telemetry |
   113→
   114→### config llm
   115→
   116→```bash
   117→code-insights config llm [options]
   118→```
   119→
   120→Configure the LLM provider for AI-powered session analysis. Without flags, runs an interactive wizard.
   121→
   122→| Flag | Description |
   123→|------|-------------|
   124→| `--provider <provider>` | LLM provider (`openai`, `anthropic`, `gemini`, `ollama`) |
   125→| `--model <model>` | Model ID (e.g., `gpt-4o`, `claude-sonnet-4-20250514`) |
   126→| `--api-key <key>` | API key for the selected provider |
   127→| `--base-url <url>` | Custom base URL (for Ollama or local endpoints) |
   128→| `--show` | Show current LLM configuration |
   129→
   130→## install-hook
   131→
   132→```bash
   133→code-insights install-hook
   134→```
   135→
   136→Adds a `Stop` hook to `~/.claude/settings.json` that runs `code-insights sync -q` after every Claude Code session.
   137→
   138→## uninstall-hook
   139→
   140→```bash
   141→code-insights uninstall-hook
   142→```
   143→
   144→Removes the auto-sync hook from `~/.claude/settings.json`.
   145→
   146→## reset
   147→
   148→```bash
   149→code-insights reset --confirm
   150→```
   151→
   152→Deletes all local data: `~/.code-insights/data.db`, `sync-state.json`, and `device-id`. Requires the `--confirm` flag to prevent accidental deletion.
   153→
   154→## telemetry
   155→
   156→```bash
   157→code-insights telemetry [subcommand]
   158→```
   159→
   160→View and manage anonymous telemetry settings.
   161→
   162→| Subcommand | Description |
   163→|------------|-------------|
   164→| `status` | Show current telemetry status and what data is collected |
   165→| `opt-out` | Disable anonymous telemetry |
   166→| `opt-in` | Re-enable anonymous telemetry |
   167→
   168→Telemetry can also be disabled via environment variables: `CODE_INSIGHTS_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1` or `DO_NOT_TRACK=1`.
   169→

1→---
     2→title: "Database Schema"
     3→description: "Reference for the Code Insights SQLite database schema — projects, sessions, messages, and insights tables at ~/.code-insights/data.db."
     4→---
     5→
     6→The local SQLite database is stored at `~/.code-insights/data.db`. It uses WAL mode for concurrent reads during CLI sync. All timestamps are ISO 8601 strings. Arrays and nested objects are stored as JSON strings.
     7→
     8→## Tables
     9→
    10→### projects
    11→
    12→Stores project metadata. The project ID is derived from the git remote URL (stable across devices) or a hash of the working directory path.
    13→
    14→| Column | Type | Default | Description |
    15→|--------|------|---------|-------------|
    16→| `id` | TEXT PRIMARY KEY | — | Hash of git remote URL or directory path |
    17→| `name` | TEXT NOT NULL | — | Project display name |
    18→| `path` | TEXT NOT NULL | — | Working directory path |
    19→| `git_remote_url` | TEXT | NULL | Git remote URL (nullable) |
    20→| `project_id_source` | TEXT NOT NULL | `'path-hash'` | How the ID was derived: `'git-remote'` or `'path-hash'` |
    21→| `session_count` | INTEGER NOT NULL | `0` | Number of synced sessions |
    22→| `last_activity` | TEXT NOT NULL | — | ISO 8601 timestamp of most recent session |
    23→| `created_at` | TEXT NOT NULL | `datetime('now')` | ISO 8601 timestamp |
    24→| `updated_at` | TEXT NOT NULL | `datetime('now')` | ISO 8601 timestamp |
    25→| `total_input_tokens` | INTEGER | `0` | Cumulative input tokens across all sessions |
    26→| `total_output_tokens` | INTEGER | `0` | Cumulative output tokens |
    27→| `cache_creation_tokens` | INTEGER | `0` | Cumulative cache write tokens |
    28→| `cache_read_tokens` | INTEGER | `0` | Cumulative cache read tokens |
    29→| `estimated_cost_usd` | REAL | `0` | Cumulative estimated cost in USD |
    30→
    31→### sessions
    32→
    33→Stores session metadata with generated titles, character classification, token usage, and device info.
    34→
    35→| Column | Type | Default | Description |
    36→|--------|------|---------|-------------|
    37→| `id` | TEXT PRIMARY KEY | — | Session ID from source tool |
    38→| `project_id` | TEXT NOT NULL | — | Foreign key to `projects.id` |
    39→| `project_name` | TEXT NOT NULL | — | Denormalized project name |
    40→| `project_path` | TEXT NOT NULL | — | Denormalized project path |
    41→| `git_remote_url` | TEXT | NULL | Git remote URL at sync time |
    42→| `summary` | TEXT | NULL | LLM-generated session summary |
    43→| `custom_title` | TEXT | NULL | User-provided title |
    44→| `generated_title` | TEXT | NULL | Auto-generated title |
    45→| `title_source` | TEXT | NULL | Which tier generated the title |
    46→| `session_character` | TEXT | NULL | Classification: `deep_focus`, `bug_hunt`, `feature_build`, `exploration`, `refactor`, `learning`, `quick_task` |
    47→| `started_at` | TEXT NOT NULL | — | ISO 8601 session start time |
    48→| `ended_at` | TEXT NOT NULL | — | ISO 8601 session end time |
    49→| `message_count` | INTEGER NOT NULL | `0` | Total messages |
    50→| `user_message_count` | INTEGER NOT NULL | `0` | User messages only |
    51→| `assistant_message_count` | INTEGER NOT NULL | `0` | Assistant messages only |
    52→| `tool_call_count` | INTEGER NOT NULL | `0` | Total tool calls |
    53→| `git_branch` | TEXT | NULL | Git branch at session time |
    54→| `claude_version` | TEXT | NULL | Claude version string (Claude Code only) |
    55→| `source_tool` | TEXT NOT NULL | `'claude-code'` | `claude-code`, `cursor`, `codex-cli`, `copilot-cli` |
    56→| `device_id` | TEXT | NULL | Stable anonymous device identifier |
    57→| `device_hostname` | TEXT | NULL | Machine hostname |
    58→| `device_platform` | TEXT | NULL | `darwin`, `linux`, `win32` |
    59→| `synced_at` | TEXT NOT NULL | `datetime('now')` | ISO 8601 sync timestamp |
    60→| `total_input_tokens` | INTEGER | NULL | Total input tokens for session |
    61→| `total_output_tokens` | INTEGER | NULL | Total output tokens |
    62→| `cache_creation_tokens` | INTEGER | NULL | Cache write tokens |
    63→| `cache_read_tokens` | INTEGER | NULL | Cache read tokens |
    64→| `estimated_cost_usd` | REAL | NULL | Estimated cost in USD |
    65→| `models_used` | TEXT | NULL | JSON array of all model IDs used |
    66→| `primary_model` | TEXT | NULL | Most-used model ID |
    67→| `usage_source` | TEXT | NULL | How usage was calculated |
    68→
    69→**Indexes:** `project_id`, `started_at DESC`, `source_tool`
    70→
    71→### messages
    72→
    73→Stores full message content for all sessions.
    74→
    75→| Column | Type | Default | Description |
    76→|--------|------|---------|-------------|
    77→| `id` | TEXT PRIMARY KEY | — | Message ID |
    78→| `session_id` | TEXT NOT NULL | — | Foreign key to `sessions.id` |
    79→| `type` | TEXT NOT NULL | — | `user` or `assistant` |
    80→| `content` | TEXT NOT NULL | `''` | Full message content |
    81→| `thinking` | TEXT | NULL | Extended thinking content (JSON) |
    82→| `tool_calls` | TEXT | NULL | JSON array of tool calls |
    83→| `tool_results` | TEXT | NULL | JSON array of tool results |
    84→| `usage` | TEXT | NULL | JSON token usage for this message |
    85→| `timestamp` | TEXT NOT NULL | — | ISO 8601 message timestamp |
    86→| `parent_id` | TEXT | NULL | Parent message ID |
    87→
    88→**Indexes:** `session_id`, `(session_id, timestamp ASC)`
    89→
    90→### insights
    91→
    92→Stores LLM-generated insights. Written by the embedded dashboard server, not the CLI.
    93→
    94→| Column | Type | Default | Description |
    95→|--------|------|---------|-------------|
    96→| `id` | TEXT PRIMARY KEY | — | UUID |
    97→| `session_id` | TEXT NOT NULL | — | Foreign key to `sessions.id` |
    98→| `project_id` | TEXT NOT NULL | — | Foreign key to `projects.id` |
    99→| `project_name` | TEXT NOT NULL | — | Denormalized project name |
   100→| `type` | TEXT NOT NULL | — | `summary`, `decision`, `learning`, `technique`, `prompt_quality` |
   101→| `title` | TEXT NOT NULL | — | Insight title |
   102→| `content` | TEXT NOT NULL | — | Full insight text |
   103→| `summary` | TEXT NOT NULL | — | One-line summary |
   104→| `bullets` | TEXT | NULL | JSON array of bullet points |
   105→| `confidence` | REAL NOT NULL | — | Confidence score 0.0–1.0 |
   106→| `source` | TEXT NOT NULL | `'llm'` | Always `llm` |
   107→| `metadata` | TEXT | NULL | JSON metadata blob |
   108→| `timestamp` | TEXT NOT NULL | — | ISO 8601 insight timestamp |
   109→| `created_at` | TEXT NOT NULL | `datetime('now')` | ISO 8601 creation timestamp |
   110→| `scope` | TEXT NOT NULL | `'session'` | Always `session` currently |
   111→| `analysis_version` | TEXT NOT NULL | `'1.0.0'` | Analysis pipeline version |
   112→| `linked_insight_ids` | TEXT | NULL | JSON array of related insight IDs |
   113→
   114→**Indexes:** `session_id`, `project_id`, `type`, `timestamp DESC`
   115→
   116→### usage_stats
   117→
   118→A singleton row (id = 1) tracking cumulative totals across all sessions. Updated by the CLI on each sync.
   119→
   120→| Column | Type | Default | Description |
   121→|--------|------|---------|-------------|
   122→| `id` | INTEGER PRIMARY KEY | — | Always 1 (singleton) |
   123→| `total_input_tokens` | INTEGER | `0` | Cumulative input tokens |
   124→| `total_output_tokens` | INTEGER | `0` | Cumulative output tokens |
   125→| `cache_creation_tokens` | INTEGER | `0` | Cumulative cache write tokens |
   126→| `cache_read_tokens` | INTEGER | `0` | Cumulative cache read tokens |
   127→| `estimated_cost_usd` | REAL | `0` | Cumulative cost in USD |
   128→| `sessions_with_usage` | INTEGER | `0` | Sessions that have token data |
   129→| `last_updated_at` | TEXT | `datetime('now')` | ISO 8601 last update time |
   130→
   131→## Accessing the Database
   132→
   133→You can query the database directly with any SQLite client:
   134→
   135→```bash
   136→sqlite3 ~/.code-insights/data.db
   137→
   138→.tables
   139→SELECT generated_title, session_character, estimated_cost_usd FROM sessions ORDER BY started_at DESC LIMIT 10;
   140→SELECT SUM(estimated_cost_usd) FROM sessions;
   141→SELECT source_tool, COUNT(*) FROM sessions GROUP BY source_tool;
   142→```
   143→

1→---
     2→title: "Configuration"
     3→description: "All Code Insights configuration files, data paths, and local storage locations. Covers config.json, sync state, device ID, and the SQLite database."
     4→---
     5→
     6→## File locations
     7→
     8→All Code Insights data lives in `~/.code-insights/`:
     9→
    10→| File | Purpose |
    11→|------|---------|
    12→| `~/.code-insights/data.db` | SQLite database (sessions, insights, messages) |
    13→| `~/.code-insights/config.json` | CLI configuration |
    14→| `~/.code-insights/sync-state.json` | Incremental sync state (file modification times) |
    15→| `~/.code-insights/device-id` | Stable device identifier |
    16→
    17→## config.json
    18→
    19→Created by `code-insights init`. Example:
    20→
    21→```json
    22→{
    23→  "sourcePaths": {
    24→    "claudeCode": "/Users/you/.claude/projects",
    25→    "cursor": "/Users/you/Library/Application Support/Cursor/User/globalStorage"
    26→  }
    27→}
    28→```
    29→
    30→## Viewing config
    31→
    32→```bash
    33→code-insights config
    34→```
    35→
    36→Shows sync paths, dashboard port, LLM provider, and telemetry status.
    37→
    38→## LLM settings
    39→
    40→LLM provider settings (provider, model, API key, base URL) are stored in `config.json` under the `dashboard.llm` key. Configure via CLI or the dashboard Settings page:
    41→
    42→```bash
    43→# Interactive wizard
    44→code-insights config llm
    45→
    46→# Non-interactive
    47→code-insights config llm --provider openai --model gpt-4o --api-key sk-...
    48→
    49→# View current config
    50→code-insights config llm --show
    51→```
    52→
    53→Supported providers: **OpenAI**, **Anthropic**, **Google Gemini**, **Ollama** (local, no API key needed).
    54→
    55→## Telemetry
    56→
    57→Anonymous, opt-in telemetry collects command name, success/failure, CLI version, OS, and session count bucket. No filenames, project names, session content, API keys, or personal info is ever collected.
    58→
    59→```bash
    60→code-insights telemetry status     # View what's collected
    61→code-insights telemetry opt-out    # Disable
    62→code-insights telemetry opt-in     # Re-enable
    63→code-insights config set telemetry false  # Alternative
    64→```
    65→
    66→Environment variable overrides: `CODE_INSIGHTS_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1` or `DO_NOT_TRACK=1`.
    67→
    68→## Resetting
    69→
    70→To delete all local data:
    71→
    72→```bash
    73→code-insights reset --confirm
    74→```
    75→
    76→This deletes `data.db`, `sync-state.json`, and `device-id`. The `config.json` is preserved.
    77→
    78→To delete everything including config:
    79→
    80→```bash
    81→rm -rf ~/.code-insights
    82→```
    83→

1→--- 2→title: "The Dashboard" 3→description: "Tour the Code Insights embedded dashboard — session list, activity charts, project stats, insights, and chat view, all served locally at localhost:7890." 4→--- 5→ 6→## Launching the dashboard 7→ 8→```bash 9→code-insights dashboard 10→``` 11→ 12→Opens your browser at `localhost:7890`. The dashboard is a Vite + React SPA served by the Hono server embedded in the CLI. It runs as long as the terminal process is alive. 13→ 14→ 15→ 16→ 17→ 18→ 19→## Navigation 20→ 21→| Page | What it shows | 22→|------|---------------| 23→| Dashboard | Activity chart, session stats, unanalyzed session count, bulk analyze button | 24→| Sessions | Three-panel session browser with project nav, session list, and detail panel with Insights/Prompt Quality/Conversation tabs | 25→| Insights | All AI-generated insights in a compact, collapsible list with Timeline/By Type/By Project/By Session views and recurring pattern detection | 26→| Analytics | Charts: activity over time, model usage, cost breakdown, project table | 27→| Export | Download sessions and insights as Markdown (by project or date range) | 28→| Settings | LLM provider configuration with credential testing and Ollama model discovery | 29→ 30→## LLM configuration 31→ 32→ 33→ 34→ 35→ 36→ 37→To enable AI-powered insights: 38→ 39→1. Open **Settings** in the dashboard 40→2. Select a provider: OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, or Ollama 41→3. Enter your API key 42→4. Return to any session and click **Analyze** 43→ 44→Ollama runs fully locally — no API key required. Install Ollama and start a model: 45→ 46→```bash 47→ollama pull llama3.2 48→``` 49→ 50→Then select **Ollama** in Settings and enter your model name. 51→ 52→## Port configuration 53→ 54→If port 7890 is already in use, the dashboard will fail to start. Check what's using the port: 55→ 56→```bash 57→lsof -i :7890 58→``` 59→

1→---
     2→title: "Multi-Source Setup"
     3→description: "Set up Code Insights with multiple AI coding tools — Claude Code, Cursor, Codex CLI, Copilot CLI, and VS Code Copilot Chat. All auto-detected."
     4→---
     5→
     6→## Which tools are supported?
     7→
     8→All five tools are detected automatically — no per-tool configuration needed.
     9→
    10→| Tool | Session location | Auto-detected |
    11→|------|-----------------|---------------|
    12→| Claude Code | `~/.claude/projects/**/*.jsonl` | Yes |
    13→| Cursor | Platform workspace storage | Yes |
    14→| Codex CLI | `~/.codex/sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/rollout-*.jsonl` | Yes |
    15→| Copilot CLI | `~/.copilot/session-state/{id}/events.jsonl` | Yes |
    16→| VS Code Copilot Chat | VS Code `workspaceStorage/` chat sessions | Yes |
    17→
    18→## How do I sync a specific tool?
    19→
    20→```bash
    21→code-insights sync --source claude-code
    22→code-insights sync --source cursor
    23→code-insights sync --source codex-cli
    24→code-insights sync --source copilot-cli
    25→code-insights sync --source copilot
    26→```
    27→
    28→## How do I filter stats by tool?
    29→
    30→```bash
    31→code-insights stats --source claude-code
    32→code-insights stats cost --source cursor
    33→code-insights stats today --source codex-cli
    34→```
    35→
    36→## How do I tell which tool a session came from?
    37→
    38→The dashboard shows a source tool badge on each session card so you can quickly identify which tool produced each session.
    39→
    40→## Where are session files stored on each platform?
    41→
    42→**Cursor** session storage varies by platform:
    43→- macOS: `~/Library/Application Support/Cursor/User/globalStorage/`
    44→- Linux: `~/.config/Cursor/User/globalStorage/`
    45→- Windows: `%APPDATA%\Cursor\User\globalStorage\`
    46→
    47→**Claude Code** sessions are stored in JSONL format under `~/.claude/projects/`. Each project directory corresponds to a working directory path.
    48→
    49→**VS Code Copilot Chat** sessions are stored as JSON files under VS Code's `workspaceStorage/` directory. Each chat conversation is a standalone file discovered automatically.
    50→

1→--- 2→title: "Sessions & Chat" 3→description: "Browse your AI coding sessions, view full conversation history with tool calls, and filter by project, source tool, or session character." 4→--- 5→ 6→## Session list 7→ 8→The **Sessions** page uses a three-panel layout: project navigation on the left, session list in the middle, and session detail on the right. 9→ 10→Each session row shows: 11→ 12→- Session title (auto-generated from conversation content) 13→- Source tool badge (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex CLI, Copilot CLI) 14→- Date and duration 15→- Message count, cost, and insight counts 16→- Session character badge (classification) 17→- Prompt quality score (when analyzed) 18→ 19→ 20→ 21→ 22→ 23→ 24→## Session characters 25→ 26→Each session is classified into one of 7 types. Classification happens in two ways: 27→ 28→1. **During sync** — a heuristic based on tool call patterns provides an initial classification 29→2. **During LLM analysis** — the LLM classifies based on conversation content, providing more accurate results 30→ 31→The LLM classification overrides the heuristic when analysis is run. 32→ 33→| Character | Description | 34→|-----------|-------------| 35→| `deep_focus` | Long, concentrated work on a specific problem or area | 36→| `bug_hunt` | Debugging-driven — investigating errors, tracing issues, fixing bugs | 37→| `feature_build` | Building new functionality — creating files, adding endpoints, wiring components | 38→| `exploration` | Research-oriented — reading code, searching, understanding before acting | 39→| `refactor` | Restructuring existing code — renaming, moving, reorganizing without new features | 40→| `learning` | Knowledge-seeking — asking questions, understanding concepts, getting explanations | 41→| `quick_task` | Short and focused — small fix, config change, or one-off task | 42→ 43→## Session detail 44→ 45→Click any session to open the detail view with three tabs: 46→ 47→### Insights tab 48→ 49→- **Vitals strip** — duration (with time range), messages (user/assistant split), tokens (in/cache/out breakdown), cost (with model name) 50→- **Summary** — AI-generated narrative with outcome status and key bullets (requires analysis) 51→- **Pull Requests** — GitHub PR links referenced in the conversation, shown as clickable badges 52→- **Learnings** — collapsible items with root cause analysis, color-coded with green accent 53→- **Decisions** — collapsible items with situation/choice/reasoning, color-coded with blue accent 54→ 55→### Prompt Quality tab 56→ 57→- **Efficiency score** — 0-100 score with progress ring and tier label (Excellent/Good/Needs Improvement/Poor) 58→- **Score badge** — colored score shown in the tab header for quick scanning 59→- **Session traits, anti-patterns, wasted turns, and tips** — full prompt quality analysis 60→- If not yet analyzed, shows an inline Analyze button 61→ 62→### Conversation tab 63→ 64→ 65→ 66→ 67→ 68→ 69→- All messages in chronological order 70→- Tool calls shown inline with parameters and results 71→- Thinking blocks (Claude Code only) 72→- Code blocks with syntax highlighting 73→ 74→## Searching and filtering 75→ 76→Use the search bar to find sessions by title. Filter by: 77→ 78→- **Project** — select from the left panel (grouped by git remote or path) 79→- **Character** — session type (Deep Focus, Bug Hunt, etc.) 80→- **Status** — analyzed or not analyzed 81→- **Source tool** — Claude Code, Cursor, Codex CLI, Copilot CLI (in the project nav panel) 82→ 83→Sessions are grouped by date (Today, Yesterday, This Week, etc.) with daily separators. 84→ 85→## Renaming sessions 86→ 87→The auto-generated title can be customized. Click the pencil icon next to the title in the detail view to rename it. When analysis is run, the AI may also suggest a better title — you can apply or dismiss the suggestion. 88→

1→---
     2→title: "Syncing Sessions"
     3→description: "Sync your AI coding sessions to a local SQLite database. Supports auto-sync, source filtering, force re-sync, and dry-run mode across all tools."
     4→---
     5→
     6→## How do I sync my sessions?
     7→
     8→```bash
     9→code-insights sync
    10→```
    11→
    12→Discovers sessions from all supported tools and syncs new or updated sessions to the local SQLite database. The first sync processes your entire history; subsequent syncs are incremental.
    13→
    14→## How do I sync from a specific tool?
    15→
    16→```bash
    17→code-insights sync --source claude-code
    18→code-insights sync --source cursor
    19→code-insights sync --source codex-cli
    20→code-insights sync --source copilot-cli
    21→code-insights sync --source copilot
    22→```
    23→
    24→## How do I force a re-sync?
    25→
    26→```bash
    27→code-insights sync --force
    28→```
    29→
    30→Re-processes all sessions even if they haven't changed. Useful after a CLI update.
    31→
    32→## Dry run
    33→
    34→```bash
    35→code-insights sync --dry-run
    36→```
    37→
    38→Shows what would be synced without writing to the database.
    39→
    40→## Quiet mode
    41→
    42→```bash
    43→code-insights sync -q
    44→```
    45→
    46→Suppresses all output. Used by the auto-sync hook.
    47→
    48→## How do I set up auto-sync?
    49→
    50→Install a hook that automatically syncs when a Claude Code session ends:
    51→
    52→```bash
    53→code-insights install-hook
    54→```
    55→
    56→To remove it:
    57→
    58→```bash
    59→code-insights uninstall-hook
    60→```
    61→
    62→The hook adds a `Stop` hook to `~/.claude/settings.json` that runs `code-insights sync -q` after every Claude Code session.
    63→
    64→## Troubleshooting
    65→
    66→**No sessions found** — Check that your AI coding tool has been used and that session files exist in the expected location. Run `code-insights status` to see what the CLI has discovered.
    67→
    68→**SQLite locked error** — Another process is using the database. Wait a moment and retry.
    69→

1→--- 2→title: "Terminal Analytics" 3→description: "View cost breakdowns, model usage, project activity, and today's sessions directly in your terminal with the code-insights stats command suite." 4→--- 5→ 6→## Overview 7→ 8→```bash 9→code-insights stats 10→``` 11→ 12→Shows a dashboard overview for the last 7 days: session count, messages, tool calls, duration, cost, and an activity chart. 13→ 14→ 15→ 16→## Subcommands 17→ 18→### Cost breakdown 19→ 20→```bash 21→code-insights stats cost 22→``` 23→ 24→Cost breakdown by project and model. Shows token usage, cache hits, and USD cost. 25→ 26→### Per-project details 27→ 28→```bash 29→code-insights stats projects 30→``` 31→ 32→Detail cards for each project: session count, duration, character distribution. 33→ 34→### Today's sessions 35→ 36→```bash 37→code-insights stats today 38→``` 39→ 40→All sessions from today with titles and durations. 41→ 42→### Model usage 43→ 44→```bash 45→code-insights stats models 46→``` 47→ 48→Model usage distribution: session count, message count, and cost per model. 49→ 50→## Shared flags 51→ 52→All stats subcommands support these flags: 53→ 54→| Flag | Default | Description | 55→|------|---------|-------------| 56→| `--period 7d\|30d\|90d\|all` | `7d` | Time range | 57→| `--project ` | — | Filter to a specific project | 58→| `--source ` | — | Filter by source tool | 59→| `--no-sync` | — | Skip auto-sync before showing stats | 60→ 61→## Examples 62→ 63→```bash 64→# Cost for the last 30 days 65→code-insights stats cost --period 30d 66→ 67→# Today's sessions for a specific project 68→code-insights stats today --project my-app 69→ 70→# Model usage for Cursor sessions only 71→code-insights stats models --source cursor 72→``` 73→

1→--- 2→title: "Insights & Analysis" 3→description: "Use LLM-powered analysis to extract decisions, learnings, techniques, and patterns from your AI coding sessions. Supports OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, and Ollama." 4→--- 5→ 6→## What are Code Insights insights? 7→ 8→When you analyze a session, the CLI server sends the conversation to your configured LLM provider and extracts structured insights. These are stored in the local SQLite database and displayed in the dashboard. 9→ 10→The analysis uses a "senior staff engineer writing for a knowledge base" persona — each insight is written as if a future developer will read it 6 months from now with no context about the session. 11→ 12→ 13→ 14→ 15→ 16→ 17→## What types of insights are extracted? 18→ 19→| Type | What it captures | 20→|------|-----------------| 21→| **Summary** | Narrative of what was accomplished, outcome status (success/partial/abandoned/blocked), and key artifacts changed | 22→| **Decision** | A technical choice with full context: situation, reasoning, rejected alternatives, trade-offs, and conditions for revisiting | 23→| **Learning** | A technical discovery: observable symptom, root cause, transferable takeaway, and when it applies | 24→| **Technique** | A reusable approach or pattern demonstrated | 25→| **Prompt Quality** | Efficiency score, wasted turns, anti-patterns, and session behavioral traits | 26→ 27→### Decision structure 28→ 29→Each decision insight includes: 30→ 31→- **Situation** — what problem or requirement led to the decision 32→- **Choice** — what was chosen and how it was implemented 33→- **Reasoning** — the key factors that tipped the decision 34→- **Alternatives** — rejected options with reasons why 35→- **Trade-offs** — what downsides were accepted 36→- **Revisit when** — conditions under which to reconsider 37→ 38→### Learning structure 39→ 40→Each learning insight includes: 41→ 42→- **Symptom** — what went wrong or was confusing 43→- **Root cause** — the underlying technical reason 44→- **Takeaway** — the transferable lesson for similar situations 45→- **Applies when** — conditions under which this knowledge is relevant 46→ 47→## How do I run analysis on a session? 48→ 49→1. Open the dashboard: `code-insights dashboard` 50→2. Navigate to **Sessions** 51→3. Click into any session 52→4. Click **Analyze** (requires LLM configured in Settings) 53→ 54→Analysis runs server-side via the Hono API. Progress is streamed back to the dashboard in real time via Server-Sent Events (SSE), so you can see each insight as it's extracted. Your API key stays on your machine and is never sent anywhere else. 55→ 56→During analysis, the LLM also classifies the session into one of 7 character types (deep_focus, bug_hunt, feature_build, etc.) and writes it back to the session record. 57→ 58→## How do I analyze multiple sessions at once? 59→ 60→From the **Dashboard** page, click **Analyze** on the unanalyzed sessions card to run analysis across all pending sessions. You can also analyze individual sessions from the session detail page. Progress is shown in real time via SSE streaming. 61→ 62→## How does prompt quality scoring work? 63→ 64→The prompt quality analysis evaluates your prompting efficiency on a 0-100 scale. 65→ 66→### What it detects 67→ 68→- **Wasted turns** — messages that led to unnecessary clarifications or corrections, with the original message, what went wrong, and a suggested rewrite 69→- **Anti-patterns** — recurring bad habits (e.g., vague instructions, missing file paths) with specific fixes 70→- **Session traits** — higher-level behavioral patterns about how the session was structured: 71→ 72→| Trait | What it means | 73→|-------|--------------| 74→| `context_drift` | Session covers too many unrelated objectives, causing the AI to lose context | 75→| `objective_bloat` | Too many different tasks crammed into one session | 76→| `late_context` | Critical requirements provided late that should have been mentioned upfront | 77→| `no_planning` | Jumped straight into implementation without discussing the approach | 78→| `good_structure` | Well-structured session with clear objectives and logical flow | 79→ 80→Each trait includes a severity (high/medium/low), evidence from the conversation, and a suggestion for improvement. 81→ 82→## Where are insights stored? 83→ 84→All insights are stored in the `insights` table in `~/.code-insights/data.db`. You can export individual sessions as Markdown from the session detail panel, or use the **Export** page to generate AI-synthesized knowledge across sessions. See [Analytics & Export](/docs/guides/analytics-export) for details. 85→

1→---
     2→title: "Analytics & Export"
     3→description: "View activity charts and cost analytics. Export session insights with quick Markdown downloads or AI-powered synthesis across sessions."
     4→---
     5→
     6→## Analytics dashboard
     7→
     8→The **Analytics** page provides charts of your coding activity:
     9→
    10→- **Activity over time** — sessions per day or week
    11→- **Model usage** — sessions and messages by model
    12→- **Session characters** — distribution of session types (deep focus, bug hunt, feature build, etc.)
    13→- **Cost trends** — spending over time by project and model
    14→- **Tool call patterns** — which tools you use most
    15→
    16→All charts respond to the period selector: **7 days**, **30 days**, **90 days**, or **all time**.
    17→
    18→---
    19→
    20→## Quick session export
    21→
    22→Export a single session directly from the session detail panel.
    23→
    24→1. Open the dashboard and navigate to **Sessions**
    25→2. Click into any session to open the detail view
    26→3. Click the **download icon** (↓) in the panel header
    27→4. Choose a format from the dropdown:
    28→
    29→| Format | Output |
    30→|--------|--------|
    31→| **Markdown** | Standard `.md` with session metadata, summary, and insights |
    32→| **Obsidian** | Markdown with YAML frontmatter for Obsidian vaults |
    33→| **Notion** | Markdown formatted for Notion import |
    34→
    35→The export includes the session title, date, duration, project, summary, and all extracted insights (decisions, learnings, techniques, prompt quality).
    36→
    37→<Callout type="tip">
    38→Run analysis on a session first for richer exports. Without analysis, the export contains only basic session metadata.
    39→</Callout>
    40→
    41→---
    42→
    43→## AI-powered export
    44→
    45→The **Export** page uses your configured LLM to synthesize insights across multiple sessions into curated, deduplicated knowledge. Unlike quick exports, the AI compiles knowledge — it doesn't just concatenate raw data.
    46→
    47→<Callout type="note">
    48→AI-powered export requires an LLM provider configured in **Settings**. Supports OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, and Ollama.
    49→</Callout>
    50→
    51→### The 4-step wizard
    52→
    53→<Steps>
    54→<Step title="Choose scope">
    55→
    56→Select what to include:
    57→
    58→- **Project** — insights from a single project. All output rules are implicitly scoped to that project.
    59→- **All projects** — insights across every project. The AI labels each rule as **UNIVERSAL** (applies everywhere) or **PROJECT-SPECIFIC** (applies only in a certain context).
    60→
    61→</Step>
    62→<Step title="Configure format and depth">
    63→
    64→Pick an **output format** and a **depth preset**. A stat bar shows how many insights are available for your selected scope.
    65→
    66→</Step>
    67→<Step title="Generate">
    68→
    69→Click **Generate with AI**. Progress streams in real time:
    70→1. Loading insights from the database
    71→2. Sending to your LLM for synthesis
    72→3. Generation complete — auto-advances to the next step
    73→
    74→You can **cancel** mid-generation to save tokens.
    75→
    76→</Step>
    77→<Step title="Review and export">
    78→
    79→Preview the generated content, then:
    80→
    81→- **Copy to clipboard** — paste directly into your editor
    82→- **Download as .md** — saves a Markdown file
    83→
    84→Click **Start over** to try a different format or scope.
    85→
    86→</Step>
    87→</Steps>
    88→
    89→### Output formats
    90→
    91→| Format | Best for | What it generates |
    92→|--------|----------|-------------------|
    93→| **Agent Rules** | `CLAUDE.md`, `.cursorrules`, agent configs | Imperative instructions with WHEN conditions and REVISIT triggers. Includes a "Prompt Hygiene" section. |
    94→| **Knowledge Brief** | Documentation, wikis, team onboarding | Structured markdown with executive summary, decisions, learnings, and techniques organized by topic. |
    95→| **Obsidian** | Obsidian vaults | Markdown with YAML frontmatter and `[[wikilinks]]` for cross-referencing between notes. |
    96→| **Notion** | Notion pages | Markdown with toggle blocks, callout blocks, and tables — optimized for Notion's block model. |
    97→
    98→### Depth presets
    99→
   100→| Depth | Insights included | Use case |
   101→|-------|-------------------|----------|
   102→| **Essential** | ~25 top insights | Quick reference. Top rules only — fast and focused. |
   103→| **Standard** (default) | ~80 insights | Key decisions, learnings, and techniques. Good balance of coverage and conciseness. |
   104→| **Comprehensive** | ~200 insights | Everything available. Most thorough — useful for full project knowledge dumps. |
   105→
   106→### What the AI does
   107→
   108→The LLM doesn't just reformat — it compiles knowledge:
   109→
   110→- **Deduplicates** overlapping learnings across sessions
   111→- **Resolves conflicts** between decisions made at different times (notes the evolution)
   112→- **Prioritizes** by frequency and confidence score
   113→- **Contextualizes** each rule with WHEN conditions for applicability
   114→- **Classifies scope** (in All mode) — labels rules as UNIVERSAL or PROJECT-SPECIFIC
   115→
   116→### Example: Agent Rules output
   117→
   118→```markdown
   119→## Architecture
   120→
   121→- ALWAYS use SQLite with WAL mode for local-first applications
   122→  - WHEN: Building CLI tools or desktop apps that need persistent storage
   123→  - REVISIT WHEN: Multi-device sync becomes a requirement
   124→
   125→- PREFER pnpm workspaces for monorepo management over npm/yarn
   126→  - WHEN: Project has 2+ packages with shared dependencies
   127→
   128→## Prompt Hygiene
   129→
   130→- PROVIDE file paths and line numbers when asking for code changes
   131→  - BAD: "Fix the bug in the auth module"
   132→  - GOOD: "Fix the null check in src/auth/middleware.ts:45"
   133→```
   134→

1→---
     2→title: "Troubleshooting"
     3→description: "Solutions for common Code Insights issues — sync failures, database errors, dashboard not loading, missing sessions, and provider detection problems."
     4→---
     5→
     6→## Sync issues
     7→
     8→### No sessions found
     9→
    10→Run `code-insights status` to see what the CLI discovered. If the count is 0:
    11→
    12→- Verify you have at least one AI coding tool installed and have used it
    13→- Check that the expected session files exist:
    14→  - Claude Code: `ls ~/.claude/projects/`
    15→  - Codex CLI: `ls ~/.codex/sessions/`
    16→  - Copilot CLI: `ls ~/.copilot/session-state/`
    17→
    18→### Slow first sync
    19→
    20→The first sync processes your entire history. If you have many sessions, this can take several minutes. Subsequent syncs are incremental.
    21→
    22→### SQLite locked error
    23→
    24→Another process is accessing the database. Wait a moment and retry. If the error persists, check for stuck processes:
    25→
    26→```bash
    27→lsof ~/.code-insights/data.db
    28→```
    29→
    30→### Migration error on startup
    31→
    32→If the CLI says "migration failed", try resetting the database:
    33→
    34→```bash
    35→code-insights reset --confirm
    36→code-insights sync
    37→```
    38→
    39→## Dashboard issues
    40→
    41→### Port already in use
    42→
    43→Port 7890 is taken by another process. Find it:
    44→
    45→```bash
    46→lsof -i :7890
    47→```
    48→
    49→Kill it or wait for it to finish, then retry `code-insights dashboard`.
    50→
    51→### Blank page when opening dashboard
    52→
    53→1. Check that the CLI process is still running in your terminal
    54→2. Try a hard refresh: Cmd+Shift+R (macOS) or Ctrl+Shift+R (Linux)
    55→3. Check the terminal for error messages
    56→
    57→### LLM analysis fails
    58→
    59→- Verify your API key is correct in Settings
    60→- Check your internet connection (required for OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini)
    61→- For Ollama: verify the service is running (`ollama serve`) and the model is installed
    62→
    63→### Ollama CORS error
    64→
    65→Ollama requires CORS configuration to accept requests from `localhost:7890`:
    66→
    67→```bash
    68→OLLAMA_ORIGINS="http://localhost:7890" ollama serve
    69→```
    70→
    71→## Hook issues
    72→
    73→### Auto-sync hook not triggering
    74→
    75→Check that the hook is installed:
    76→
    77→```bash
    78→cat ~/.claude/settings.json | grep code-insights
    79→```
    80→
    81→If not present, run `code-insights install-hook` again.
    82→
    83→### Wrong path in hook
    84→
    85→If you installed Code Insights via npm and the hook uses a different path, reinstall it:
    86→
    87→```bash
    88→code-insights uninstall-hook
    89→code-insights install-hook
    90→```
    91→
    92→## Getting help
    93→
    94→If you can't resolve an issue, [open a GitHub Issue](https://github.com/melagiri/code-insights/issues) with:
    95→
    96→- Your OS and Node.js version
    97→- The full error message
    98→- Output of `code-insights --version`
    99→

1→---
     2→title: "Migrating from v2"
     3→description: "Migrate from Code Insights v2 (Firebase/Bring-Your-Own-Firebase) to v3's local-first SQLite architecture. Step-by-step upgrade guide."
     4→---
     5→
     6→v3 is a complete architectural change. The dashboard, which was a hosted web app at `code-insights.app`, is now an embedded local server. Firebase and Supabase are gone. All data lives in a local SQLite database.
     7→
     8→## What changed
     9→
    10→| Aspect | v2 | v3 |
    11→|--------|----|----|
    12→| Data storage | Your Firebase Firestore | Local SQLite at `~/.code-insights/data.db` |
    13→| Dashboard | Hosted at `code-insights.app` | Embedded at `localhost:7890` |
    14→| Auth | Supabase (Google/GitHub OAuth) | None required |
    15→| Setup | 9 steps (Firebase project, Firestore, keys) | 3 steps (install, sync, dashboard) |
    16→| Privacy | Your data in your Firebase | Data never leaves your machine |
    17→
    18→## Migration steps
    19→
    20→1. **Install v3:**
    21→   ```bash
    22→   npm install -g @code-insights/cli@latest
    23→   ```
    24→
    25→2. **Initialize:**
    26→   ```bash
    27→   code-insights init
    28→   ```
    29→
    30→3. **Sync sessions:**
    31→   ```bash
    32→   code-insights sync
    33→   ```
    34→   Sessions are re-discovered from your local AI coding tool files. The sync is not reading from Firebase — it re-parses the original session files.
    35→
    36→4. **Open the dashboard:**
    37→   ```bash
    38→   code-insights dashboard
    39→   ```
    40→
    41→## Your Firebase data
    42→
    43→Your Firebase Firestore data is untouched. v3 doesn't access Firebase at all. If you want to keep both, you can — v3 and v2 are completely independent.
    44→
    45→If you want to delete your Firebase data, do so through the Firebase Console.
    46→
    47→## The `code-insights.app` dashboard
    48→
    49→The hosted web dashboard at `code-insights.app` has been repurposed as a marketing and documentation site. It no longer serves the session dashboard. All dashboard functionality is now in the embedded local dashboard at `localhost:7890`.
    50→

1→---
     2→title: "Contributing"
     3→description: "Set up the Code Insights development environment, understand the monorepo structure (CLI, dashboard, server), and submit your first contribution."
     4→---
     5→
     6→## Repository structure
     7→
     8→Code Insights is a pnpm workspace monorepo at [github.com/melagiri/code-insights](https://github.com/melagiri/code-insights).
     9→
    10→```
    11→code-insights/
    12→├── cli/           # Node.js CLI (Commander.js, SQLite, providers)
    13→├── dashboard/     # Vite + React SPA (embedded dashboard)
    14→└── server/        # Hono API server (serves dashboard + REST API)
    15→```
    16→
    17→## Prerequisites
    18→
    19→- Node.js 18+
    20→- pnpm (`npm install -g pnpm`)
    21→
    22→## Setup
    23→
    24→```bash
    25→git clone https://github.com/melagiri/code-insights
    26→cd code-insights
    27→pnpm install
    28→```
    29→
    30→## Development
    31→
    32→```bash
    33→# CLI (watch mode)
    34→cd cli && pnpm dev
    35→
    36→# Dashboard (Vite dev server)
    37→cd dashboard && pnpm dev
    38→
    39→# Server
    40→cd server && pnpm dev
    41→```
    42→
    43→## Building
    44→
    45→```bash
    46→# Build all packages from repo root
    47→pnpm build
    48→```
    49→
    50→## Testing the CLI locally
    51→
    52→```bash
    53→cd cli
    54→pnpm build
    55→npm link
    56→code-insights --version
    57→```
    58→
    59→## Project conventions
    60→
    61→- TypeScript strict mode, ES Modules
    62→- CLI binary: `code-insights`
    63→- npm package: `@code-insights/cli`
    64→- Dashboard uses shadcn/ui (New York style) + Tailwind CSS 4
    65→- SQLite schema changes require versioned migrations in `cli/src/db/`
    66→
    67→## Submitting changes
    68→
    69→1. Fork the repository
    70→2. Create a feature branch: `git checkout -b feature/my-feature`
    71→3. Commit changes with descriptive messages
    72→4. Push and open a pull request
    73→
    74→All PRs require a passing `pnpm build` across the workspace.
    75→

1→---
     2→title: "FAQ"
     3→description: "Frequently asked questions about Code Insights — installation, privacy, supported tools, costs, and troubleshooting."
     4→---
     5→
     6→Code Insights is a free, open-source CLI tool that parses AI coding sessions from multiple tools into a local knowledge base. Here are the most common questions.
     7→
     8→## What is Code Insights?
     9→
    10→Code Insights is an open-source CLI and embedded dashboard that analyzes your AI coding sessions. It parses session history from Claude Code, Cursor, Codex CLI, Copilot CLI, and VS Code Copilot Chat, stores everything in a local SQLite database, and provides terminal analytics plus a browser-based dashboard with optional LLM-powered insights.
    11→
    12→## Which AI coding tools does Code Insights support?
    13→
    14→Code Insights supports **five tools** out of the box:
    15→
    16→- **Claude Code** — parses JSONL session files from `~/.claude/projects/`
    17→- **Cursor** — reads the SQLite state database (`state.vscdb`)
    18→- **Codex CLI** — parses rollout JSONL files from `~/.codex/sessions/`
    19→- **Copilot CLI** — reads event JSONL files from `~/.copilot/session-state/`
    20→- **VS Code Copilot Chat** — reads chat session data from VS Code's storage
    21→
    22→All tools are auto-detected during `code-insights init`. No per-tool configuration needed. See the [Multi-Source Setup](/docs/guides/multi-source) guide for platform-specific details.
    23→
    24→## Is my data sent to the cloud?
    25→
    26→**No.** Code Insights is fully local-first. All session data is stored in a SQLite database on your machine at `~/.code-insights/data.db`. There are no accounts, no cloud services, and no data ever leaves your device.
    27→
    28→The only optional network activity is if you configure LLM-powered insights — in that case, session content is sent to your chosen LLM provider (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, or Ollama for fully local inference).
    29→
    30→## How do I install Code Insights?
    31→
    32→```bash
    33→npm install -g @code-insights/cli
    34→code-insights init
    35→code-insights sync
    36→code-insights dashboard
    37→```
    38→
    39→That's it. No configuration files, API keys, or cloud accounts needed. See the [Installation](/docs/getting-started/installation) and [Quick Start](/docs/getting-started/quick-start) guides for details.
    40→
    41→## Is Code Insights free?
    42→
    43→Yes. Code Insights is free and open-source under the [MIT license](https://github.com/melagiri/code-insights/blob/master/LICENSE). The entire stack — CLI, embedded dashboard, and Hono server — is available on [GitHub](https://github.com/melagiri/code-insights).
    44→
    45→## How does Code Insights track AI coding costs?
    46→
    47→The `code-insights stats cost` command shows cost breakdowns by project and model. Cost data is calculated from token usage in your session files. You can filter by time period (`--period 7d|30d|90d|all`), project, or source tool.
    48→
    49→The dashboard also shows cost analytics on the Analytics page with visual charts. See [Terminal Analytics](/docs/guides/terminal-analytics) for all stats subcommands.
    50→
    51→## What insights can Code Insights extract?
    52→
    53→When you run analysis on a session, Code Insights uses your configured LLM to extract five types of structured insights:
    54→
    55→- **Decisions** — architectural and design choices made during the session
    56→- **Learnings** — new knowledge gained or patterns discovered
    57→- **Techniques** — specific coding approaches or tool usage patterns
    58→- **Summaries** — concise overviews of what happened in the session
    59→- **Prompt Quality** — assessment of how effectively you prompted the AI
    60→
    61→Insights are stored locally and searchable in the dashboard. See [Insights & Analysis](/docs/guides/insights-analysis) for details on each type.
    62→
    63→## How do I sync sessions from Cursor?
    64→
    65→Cursor sessions are auto-detected. Run `code-insights sync` and the CLI will find Cursor's `state.vscdb` database in the platform-specific location (macOS, Linux, or Windows). To sync only Cursor sessions, use `code-insights sync --source cursor`.
    66→
    67→## How do I sync sessions from Claude Code?
    68→
    69→Claude Code sessions are auto-detected from `~/.claude/projects/`. Run `code-insights sync` to process all session files. Use `code-insights sync --source claude-code` to sync only Claude Code sessions.
    70→
    71→## How do I sync sessions from VS Code Copilot Chat?
    72→
    73→VS Code Copilot Chat sessions are auto-detected from VS Code's `workspaceStorage/` directory. Run `code-insights sync` and the CLI finds chat session files automatically. Use `code-insights sync --source copilot` to sync only VS Code Copilot Chat sessions.
    74→
    75→## How do I export my insights?
    76→
    77→There are two ways to export. For a single session, open the session detail panel and click the download icon to export as Markdown, Obsidian, or Notion format. For AI-powered synthesis across sessions, open the **Export** page — it uses your configured LLM to compile deduplicated, prioritized knowledge in 4 formats (Agent Rules, Knowledge Brief, Obsidian, Notion) at 3 depth levels. See [Analytics & Export](/docs/guides/analytics-export) for details.
    78→
    79→## Can I use Code Insights without an LLM API key?
    80→
    81→Yes. LLM analysis is optional. Without an API key, you still get full session syncing, browsing, search, cost tracking, terminal analytics, and the dashboard. The only feature that requires an LLM is the "Analyze" button for extracting insights.
    82→
    83→For fully local LLM inference, you can use [Ollama](https://ollama.com) — no API key or cloud service needed.
    84→
    85→## Where is my data stored?
    86→
    87→All data lives in `~/.code-insights/`:
    88→
    89→| File | Purpose |
    90→|------|---------|
    91→| `data.db` | SQLite database with all sessions, messages, and insights |
    92→| `config.json` | Configuration (source tools, preferences) |
    93→| `sync-state.json` | Tracks which files have been synced |
    94→| `device-id` | Anonymous device identifier |
    95→
    96→To delete everything: `code-insights reset --confirm` or `rm -rf ~/.code-insights`.
    97→

    43→/** Flat list of all nav items in order — used for prev/next computation and static generation */
    44→export const flatNav: NavItem[] = docsNav.flatMap((group) => group.items);
    45→
    46→/** Returns the previous and next page relative to the given slug */
    47→export function getPrevNext(slug: string): { prev: NavItem | null; next: NavItem | null } {
    48→  const index = flatNav.findIndex((item) => item.slug === slug);
    49→  return {
    50→    prev: index > 0 ? flatNav[index - 1] : null,
    51→    next: index < flatNav.length - 1 ? flatNav[index + 1] : null,
    52→  };
    53→}
    54→
    55→/** Returns the NavGroup that contains the given slug, or null */
    56→export function getActiveGroup(slug: string): NavGroup | null {
    57→  return docsNav.find((group) => group.items.some((item) => item.slug === slug)) ?? null;
    58→}

    10→
    11→
    12→There's a pattern that shows up when you look across AI coding session data: a surprisingly large percentage of turns produce unhelpful or partially-wrong responses. And most of the time, it's not a model failure — it's a prompting failure. The context was vague, the scope was wrong, or the tool was missing information it needed to give a good answer.
    13→
    14→That's actually good news. It means this is a skill you can improve.
    15→
    16→## The anatomy of a wasted turn
    17→
    18→Before we get to techniques, it helps to understand what goes wrong. Most wasted turns fall into one of three categories:
    19→
    20→**1. Vague prompts.** "Fix the bug" tells the model almost nothing. Which bug? Where? What behavior are you expecting vs. what you're seeing? Without this, the model guesses — and usually guesses wrong.

1→---
     2→title: "What Your AI Coding Sessions Reveal About Your Development Patterns"
     3→description: "Developers run hundreds of AI coding sessions but rarely look back at them. Session data reveals surprising patterns about how you actually work — and where you lose time."
     4→date: "2026-03-07"
     5→author: "Srikanth Rao"
     6→tags: ["ai-coding", "developer-patterns", "productivity"]
     7→---
     8→
     9→Most developers can tell you what they worked on today. Fewer can tell you *how* they worked on it — how many turns a task actually took, whether they explored more than they edited, or how often a "quick fix" quietly consumed an hour.
    10→
    11→That's not a character flaw. It's a tooling gap. AI coding sessions generate a surprising amount of structured data — message counts, tool calls, file touches, timestamps — but most AI coding tools don't surface session-level analytics anywhere in their UI. The terminal closes, and the data sits unused in log files.
    12→
    13→When you do look at it, patterns emerge that intuition alone doesn't surface.
    14→
    15→## The "quick task" that takes 45 minutes
    16→
    17→This is the most common pattern, and the most humbling.
    18→
    19→You open a session intending to make a small change. Rename a variable, fix a type error, update a config value. Ten minutes, tops. But the session data tells a different story: 30 messages, a dozen file reads, edits across multiple files, and a duration that quietly stretched past half an hour.
    20→

Good catch -- configuration.mdx says "opt-in" but the actual telemetry model is "opt-out" (enabled by default). Now I have everything needed.

---

Here is the complete audit with prioritized action items.

---

## Web Repo v4.0.0 Release Alignment Audit

### P0: Blocking for Release

These items contain factually wrong or missing information that will confuse users on day one.

**1. Missing `reflect` command -- entire command undocumented**
- **File:** `/Users/melagiri/Workspace/codeInsights/code-insights-web/src/content/docs/reference/cli-commands.mdx`
- **Issue:** The `reflect` command, `reflect backfill`, and `reflect backfill --prompt-quality` are completely absent. This is a major v4.0.0 feature.
- **Action:** Add full `## reflect` section documenting the command, `--week YYYY-WNN` flag, and both `backfill` subcommands.

**2. Missing `sync prune` subcommand**
- **File:** `/Users/melagiri/Workspace/codeInsights/code-insights-web/src/content/docs/reference/cli-commands.mdx`
- **Issue:** `code-insights sync prune` (soft-delete sessions with preview+confirm) is not documented.
- **Action:** Add `### sync prune` subsection under the `## sync` section.

**3. Missing `stats patterns` subcommand**
- **File:** `/Users/melagiri/Workspace/codeInsights/code-insights-web/src/content/docs/reference/cli-commands.mdx` (lines 77-84)
- **Issue:** The `stats` subcommand table lists only `cost`, `projects`, `today`, `models`. Missing `patterns`.
- **Action:** Add `patterns` row: "Cross-session patterns summary".

**4. Database schema missing 5 tables**
- **File:** `/Users/melagiri/Workspace/codeInsights/code-insights-web/src/content/docs/reference/database-schema.mdx`
- **Issue:** Documents only `projects`, `sessions`, `messages`, `insights`, and `usage_stats`. Missing:
  - `session_facets` (V5 -- stores friction points, effective patterns, facet data)
  - `reflect_snapshots` (V5 -- stores weekly reflect synthesis output)
  - `schema_version` (tracks migration version)
  - `analysis_usage` (V7 -- per-session LLM analysis cost tracking)
- **Action:** Add schema documentation for all 4 missing tables.

**5. Sessions table missing V5/V6/V7 columns**
- **File:** `/Users/melagiri/Workspace/codeInsights/code-insights-web/src/content/docs/reference/database-schema.mdx` (lines 32-68)
- **Issue:** The sessions table is missing:
  - `deleted_at` (V5 -- soft-delete column)
  - `compact_count` (V6 -- message classification)
  - `auto_compact_count` (V6 -- message classification)
  - `slash_commands` (V6 -- slash command tracking)
  - The `source_tool` column (line 55) lists `copilot-cli` as the last value but omits `copilot` (VS Code Copilot Chat)
- **Action:** Add missing columns to the sessions table documentation.

**6. Messages table missing V6 columns**
- **File:** `/Users/melagiri/Workspace/codeInsights/code-insights-web/src/content/docs/reference/database-schema.mdx` (lines 72-88)
- **Issue:** Missing V6 message classification columns (if any were added to messages -- at minimum the schema version documentation needs to reference V6 changes).
- **Action:** Verify against actual schema and add any missing columns.

**7. Telemetry described as "opt-in" -- should be "opt-out"**
- **File:** `/Users/melagiri/Workspace/codeInsights/code-insights-web/src/content/docs/reference/configuration.mdx` (line 57)
- **Issue:** Says "Anonymous, opt-in telemetry" but the actual model is opt-out (enabled by default). This is factually wrong and could damage trust.
- **Action:** Change "opt-in" to "opt-out" and clarify that telemetry is enabled by default.

**8. Quick Start omits VS Code Copilot Chat from sync description**
- **File:** `/Users/melagiri/Workspace/codeInsights/code-insights-web/src/content/docs/getting-started/quick-start.mdx` (line 20)
- **Issue:** Says "Claude Code, Cursor, Codex CLI, Copilot CLI" -- omits "VS Code Copilot Chat". This is a v3.5+ feature, not even v4, so it is already wrong today.
- **Action:** Add "VS Code Copilot Chat" to the list (or use "all five supported tools").

---

### P1: Important for Accuracy

These items describe features using stale v3 language that does not reflect the v4 reality.

**9. Prompt Quality section entirely stale -- describes old scoring model**
- **File:** `/Users/melagiri/Workspace/codeInsights/code-insights-web/src/content/docs/guides/insights-analysis.mdx` (lines 63-80)
- **Issue:** Describes prompt quality as a "0-100 efficiency score" with "wasted turns", "anti-patterns", and "session traits" (`context_drift`, `objective_bloat`, `late_context`, `no_planning`, `good_structure`). In v4, PQ was completely revised to:
  - 7 deficit categories + 3 strength categories (not traits/anti-patterns)
  - 5 dimension scores (context_provision, request_specificity, scope_management, information_timing, correction_quality)
  - Two-layer output: takeaways (before/after) + findings (categorized)
- **Action:** Rewrite the entire "How does prompt quality scoring work?" section to match the new taxonomy.

**10. Sessions & Chat page describes old PQ model**
- **File:** `/Users/melagiri/Workspace/codeInsights/code-insights-web/src/content/docs/guides/sessions-chat.mdx` (lines 56-60)
- **Issue:** "Prompt Quality tab" description says "Efficiency score -- 0-100 score with progress ring and tier label", "Session traits, anti-patterns, wasted turns, and tips". This is the old model.
- **Action:** Update to describe dimension scores, deficit/strength categories, and takeaways.

**11. Landing page ProofSection PQ card shows old model**
- **File:** `/Users/melagiri/Workspace/codeInsights/code-insights-web/src/components/marketing/ProofSection.tsx` (lines 43-57, 144-187)
- **Issue:** The `PromptQualityCard` component and its data show the old single-score model with `score: 72`, `wastedTurns: 3`, `antiPatterns`, and `traits` (`late_context`, `good_structure`). This no longer matches what users will actually see.
- **Action:** Update the mock data and card rendering to show the new dimension scores and deficit/strength categories.

**12. FeaturesSection PQ pillar description stale**
- **File:** `/Users/melagiri/Workspace/codeInsights/code-insights-web/src/components/marketing/FeaturesSection.tsx` (lines 21-30)
- **Issue:** "Prompt Coaching" pillar says "Efficiency scoring, wasted turn detection, and anti-pattern analysis. Session-level behavioral traits flag issues like context drift and late context." The bullets list "0-100 efficiency score per session", "Wasted turns with suggested rewrites", "Anti-pattern detection and fixes". All stale.
- **Action:** Update description and bullets to reference dimension scores, deficit/strength categories, and before/after takeaways.

**13. ProblemSection "after" list is stale**
- **File:** `/Users/melagiri/Workspace/codeInsights/code-insights-web/src/components/marketing/ProblemSection.tsx` (line 15)
- **Issue:** Says "Prompt quality scoring with anti-pattern detection" -- should reference the new category-based system.
- **Action:** Update to "Prompt quality analysis with deficit and strength categories" or similar.

**14. Missing Reflect & Patterns feature -- no docs page, no landing page mention**
- **Files:** 
  - `/Users/melagiri/Workspace/codeInsights/code-insights-web/src/lib/docs/docs-nav.ts` -- no Reflect guide in nav
  - `/Users/melagiri/Workspace/codeInsights/code-insights-web/src/content/docs/guides/` -- no reflect guide
  - `/Users/melagiri/Workspace/codeInsights/code-insights-web/src/components/marketing/FeaturesSection.tsx` -- no mention of Reflect/Patterns
- **Issue:** Reflect is the headline v4 feature (cross-session friction analysis, effective pattern tracking, weekly synthesis) and has zero presence on the website. The dashboard Navigation table (line 22-28 in dashboard.mdx) also omits the Patterns page.
- **Action:** 
  - Create new guide: `guides/reflect-patterns.mdx`
  - Add to docs-nav.ts in the Guides section
  - Add a 4th pillar or prominent callout in FeaturesSection for Reflect/Patterns
  - Update dashboard.mdx Navigation table to include the Patterns page

**15. Dashboard guide Navigation table missing Patterns page**
- **File:** `/Users/melagiri/Workspace/codeInsights/code-insights-web/src/content/docs/guides/dashboard.mdx` (lines 21-28)
- **Issue:** Navigation table lists Dashboard, Sessions, Insights, Analytics, Export, Settings. Missing: **Patterns** page (weekly reflect synthesis, friction categories, effective patterns).
- **Action:** Add Patterns page row to the navigation table.

**16. Introduction page missing Reflect/Patterns in feature list**
- **File:** `/Users/melagiri/Workspace/codeInsights/code-insights-web/src/content/docs/getting-started/introduction.mdx` (lines 14-21)
- **Issue:** "What can Code Insights do?" lists session browser, insights, insights page, analytics, export, terminal stats. No mention of Reflect/Patterns, friction taxonomy, or weekly synthesis.
- **Action:** Add a bullet for Reflect & Patterns (cross-session friction/pattern analysis with weekly synthesis).

**17. FAQ insight types list is incomplete**
- **File:** `/Users/melagiri/Workspace/codeInsights/code-insights-web/src/content/docs/faq.mdx` (lines 52-61)
- **Issue:** "What insights can Code Insights extract?" lists decisions, learnings, techniques, summaries, prompt quality. Does not mention friction points, effective patterns, or facets -- which are the core of the Reflect feature.
- **Action:** Add mention of cross-session analysis: friction points, effective patterns, and weekly Reflect synthesis.

**18. FAQ cost section references old `--period` flag**
- **File:** `/Users/melagiri/Workspace/codeInsights/code-insights-web/src/content/docs/faq.mdx` (line 47)
- **Issue:** Says "You can filter by time period (`--period 7d|30d|90d|all`)". The `--period` flag on `reflect` was replaced with `--week`, though `stats` may still use `--period`. Need to verify the stats command still uses `--period` (it likely does -- only `reflect` changed).
- **Action:** Verify. If stats still uses `--period`, this is fine. But the FAQ should also mention the `reflect` command's `--week` flag for cross-session analysis.

**19. Chat View missing system events documentation**
- **File:** `/Users/melagiri/Workspace/codeInsights/code-insights-web/src/content/docs/guides/sessions-chat.mdx` (lines 63-72)
- **Issue:** Conversation tab description mentions messages, tool calls, thinking blocks, and code blocks. Does not mention v4 additions: context break dividers, inline command chips, or raw message toggle.
- **Action:** Add bullets for context break dividers, inline command chips, and raw message toggle.

**20. Soft-delete / trash feature undocumented**
- **Files:** Multiple
- **Issue:** `sync prune` (covered in P0 item 2), but also the dashboard trash UI, soft-delete behavior, and the `deleted_at` column are not mentioned anywhere in the guides.
- **Action:** Add a section to syncing-sessions.mdx about pruning/soft-delete, and mention trash UI in dashboard.mdx.

**21. LLM cost tracking undocumented**
- **File:** `/Users/melagiri/Workspace/codeInsights/code-insights-web/src/content/docs/guides/insights-analysis.mdx`
- **Issue:** v4 adds per-session analysis cost transparency in the dashboard (analysis_usage table, cost shown in UI). Not mentioned anywhere in docs.
- **Action:** Add a note about analysis cost tracking in the insights-analysis guide.

**22. page.tsx FAQ JSON-LD missing Reflect**
- **File:** `/Users/melagiri/Workspace/codeInsights/code-insights-web/src/app/page.tsx` (lines 83-128)
- **Issue:** The FAQ structured data mentions "decisions, learnings, techniques, and patterns" (line 124) which is vaguely correct, but doesn't mention Reflect, friction analysis, or the weekly synthesis engine. The insight extraction answer should be updated.
- **Action:** Update the FAQ JSON-LD answer for "What insights can Code Insights extract?" to include Reflect/Patterns.

---

### P2: Nice to Have

**23. Missing "Migrating from v3 to v4" page**
- **File:** New file needed: `migrating-from-v2.mdx` is for v2->v3. No v3->v4 guide exists.
- **Issue:** v4 has auto-migrations (V5-V7) that are non-destructive and automatic. Users upgrading should know about the `--period` to `--week` change on reflect, new tables, and new features. Even though migrations are automatic, a migration guide sets expectations.
- **Action:** Create `migrating-from-v3.mdx` covering schema auto-migrations, `--period` deprecation, and new features summary.

**24. Landing page FeaturesSection could add a 4th pillar for Reflect**
- **File:** `/Users/melagiri/Workspace/codeInsights/code-insights-web/src/components/marketing/FeaturesSection.tsx`
- **Issue:** Three pillars: Knowledge Base, Prompt Coaching, Analytics & Costs. Reflect/Patterns is a distinct product layer (friction + patterns + weekly synthesis) that deserves its own pillar.
- **Action:** Either add a 4th pillar for "Cross-Session Patterns" or integrate Reflect content into the existing Knowledge Base pillar.

**25. Blog post opportunity: v4.0.0 launch post**
- **Issue:** Two existing blog posts are from early March 2026 and don't cover v4 features. A launch post would drive traffic and explain the major features.
- **Action:** Write a "What's New in Code Insights v4" blog post covering Reflect, revised taxonomies, soft-delete, cost tracking, and system events.

**26. Screenshots likely stale**
- **Files:** Referenced in dashboard.mdx, sessions-chat.mdx, insights-analysis.mdx, terminal-analytics.mdx
- **Issue:** Screenshots reference the old dashboard layout (pre-Patterns page, pre-PQ revision, pre-system events). These will look different in v4.
- **Action:** Capture new screenshots after v4 build is stable.

**27. Contributing guide missing test framework mention**
- **File:** `/Users/melagiri/Workspace/codeInsights/code-insights-web/src/content/docs/contributing.mdx`
- **Issue:** No mention of vitest or the strategic TDD policy. Doesn't mention schema migrations require idempotency tests.
- **Action:** Add a note about vitest and the testing conventions.

---

### Summary Table

| Priority | Count | Category |
|----------|-------|----------|
| P0 | 8 | Missing commands, wrong schema, wrong telemetry model |
| P1 | 14 | Stale PQ descriptions, missing Reflect feature, undocumented v4 features |
| P2 | 5 | Migration guide, blog post, screenshots, contributing |

The single highest-impact item is **P1 #14** -- Reflect & Patterns has zero presence on the website despite being the headline v4 feature. The most urgent factual errors are **P0 #7** (telemetry described as opt-in instead of opt-out) and **P0 #1-3** (missing CLI commands in the reference).

## Task: Audit code-insights-web for v4.0.0 release alignment

The main `code-insights` repo (CLI + dashboard + server) is about to ship v4.0.0. The marketing/docs website at `../code-insights-web/` needs to be updated BEFORE the npm publish so both go live together.

### Your job:
1. Read the current web repo content (landing page, docs pages, navigation) 
2. Compare against the v4.0.0 changes listed below
3. Produce a detailed, prioritized list of WHAT needs updating in the web repo

### v4.0.0 Changes Since v3.6.0 (188 commits, 50+ PRs):

**Major Features:**
1. **Reflect & Patterns** — full cross-session synthesis engine (CLI `reflect` command, `reflect backfill`, `stats patterns`, Patterns dashboard page, session_facets + reflect_snapshots tables)
2. **Friction Taxonomy** — 15→9 AI-session-focused categories with attribution model (user-actionable/ai-capability/environmental)
3. **Effective Pattern Taxonomy** — 8 canonical categories + driver field (user-driven/ai-driven/collaborative)
4. **Prompt Quality Taxonomy Revision** — scores→categories (7 deficit + 3 strength), 5 dimension scores, takeaways+findings two-layer output
5. **ISO Week Navigation** — replaced --period 7d/30d/90d with --week YYYY-WNN on reflect command
6. **Message Classification V6 Schema** — compact_count, auto_compact_count, slash_commands
7. **LLM Prompt Caching** — shared conversation prefix, ~32% input savings on Anthropic
8. **LLM Cost Tracking (V7 Schema)** — analysis_usage table, per-session cost transparency in dashboard
9. **Soft-Delete Sessions** — V5 migration, sync prune with preview+confirm, trash UI
10. **Chat View System Events** — context break dividers, inline command chips, raw message toggle
11. **Facet Backfill** — reflect backfill handles both missing and outdated sessions
12. **Data-Driven Week Navigation** — Patterns page week nav driven by actual session counts

**Schema Migrations:** V5 (soft-delete), V6 (message classification), V7 (cost tracking)

**CLI Command Changes:**
- NEW: `code-insights reflect` — cross-session LLM synthesis
- NEW: `code-insights reflect backfill` — extract facets for legacy sessions  
- NEW: `code-insights reflect backfill --prompt-quality` — backfill PQ specifically
- NEW: `code-insights stats patterns` — terminal pattern summary
- CHANGED: `reflect --week YYYY-WNN` replaced `--period 7d/30d/90d`
- NEW: `code-insights sync prune` — soft-delete sessions with preview+confirm

**Breaking Changes:**
- Schema V5-V7 auto-migrations on first run (non-destructive, automatic)
- `--period` flag on reflect replaced with `--week`
- Prompt quality metadata shape changed (handled by dual-read in dashboard)

### Web Repo Structure:
```
src/content/docs/
├── getting-started/   # introduction.mdx, installation.mdx, quick-start.mdx
├── guides/            # 7 guide pages
├── reference/         # cli-commands.mdx, database-schema.mdx, configuration.mdx
├── contributing.mdx
├── troubleshooting.mdx
└── migrating-from-v2.mdx
```

Landing page: `src/app/page.tsx`
Marketing components: `src/components/marketing/`

### Instructions:
1. READ every docs MDX file, the landing page, and marketing components
2. For each file, note what's stale/missing relative to v4
3. Produce a prioritized action list grouped by:
   - P0: Blocking for release (wrong version numbers, missing new commands, broken instructions)
   - P1: Important for accuracy (stale feature descriptions, missing new features)
   - P2: Nice to have (polish, new screenshots, blog post)
4. For each item, specify the exact file path and what needs to change

Do NOT write any code. Research only — produce the action list.
